title: Analyst/Associate
raw_description: About HASI is an investor in sustainable infrastructure assets advancing the energy transition. With more than $16 billion in managed assets, our investments are diversified across multiple asset classes, including utility-scale solar, storage, and onshore wind; distributed solar and storage; RNG; and energy efficiency. We combine deep expertise in energy markets and financial structuring with long-standing programmatic client partnerships to deliver superior risk-adjusted returns and measurable environmental benefits. HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc. is listed on the New York Stock Exchange (Ticker: HASI). For more information, please visit hasi.com. Position Summary HASI is currently seeking an Analyst/Associate – Portfolio Management to join our team in Annapolis, MD. This individual will support the review and analysis of HASI’s existing investment portfolio as well as support due diligence of new investment opportunities. Salary Range Expected salary range of $100,000-$147,500 based on experience and location. In addition, HASI offers an annual bonus program; (k) with company match; an equity incentive program; comprehensive medical, dental and vision benefits; paid time off for vacation, holidays, and sick days; and much more. title: Senior Platform Engineer
raw_description: This role is part of our ScraperAPI team, one of our growing brands at saas.group. ScraperAPI is an enterprise-scale web scraping solution, trusted by businesses across a wide range of industries to extract crucial data at scale. From e-commerce, travel to real estate and market research, we support companies in turning the web into actionable insights. We're currently on an exciting growth journey, scaling rapidly as demand for reliable, high-performance web scraping and data keeps rising. Profile Overview. We are seeking a talented Senior Platform Engineer to join our focused engineering team. We're building a product that handles billions of requests per month for businesses that depend on it. Web data is increasingly central to how companies make decisions, and our infrastructure is what makes accessing it seamless and reliable at scale. You won't be following someone else's roadmap, you'll be building it. Your immediate impact in the first 3-6 months will be:. Explore and assess the infrastructure architecture, collecting risks and opportunities that need to be addressed. Build and maintain a platform roadmap based on your findings, prioritising the work that matters most. Support the consolidation of two infrastructure stacks into a unified platform, helping shape how the migration happens. Help the engineering team increase its maturity around AI usage and standardise development environment tooling and processes. Your responsibilities. Own the platform roadmap: identi This role is part of our ScraperAPI team, one of our growing brands at saas. Own the platform roadmap: identi This role is part of our ScraperAPI team, one of our growing brands at saas. This is not a ticket-following role. Lead cost management efforts across our infrastructure, including proxy costs which are among our highest expenses. You can advise and influence technical stakeholders, adapt to different teams, and work effectively in a fully remote, async-first environment

title: Applied Research Scientist [Machine Visibility]
raw_description: Build the research function at Prerender — on a dataset no one else has. Prerender is a dynamic rendering SaaS that makes websites visible to machines — search crawlers, AI bots, and autonomous agents. Since 2017, thousands of companies have relied on our platform, which serves over 2.7 billion unique pages to web crawlers. We're part of saas.group. The web is being reshaped by machines — crawlers, LLMs, agents — and no one fully understands how. We're sitting on the data to study it, and we're working to define a new category we call machine visibility: the discipline of ensuring websites are fully accessible to the automated systems navigating the web. This role is central to that effort. The Opportunity. You'll be Prerender's first dedicated researcher, working directly with the CEO — not just reporting, but actively brainstorming, exploring questions, and shaping the research agenda together. The starting point: billions of rendered pages and crawl data spanning dozens of bot types. This data exists but hasn't been organized for systematic research. You'll build the data infrastructure that makes rigorous research possible, then use it to produce work no one else can. There's no existing research team, no inherited methodology, no playbook. You'll build it all. Some of the questions waiting to be studied:. How do GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, and other AI crawlers actually behave in production — and how do agentic sessions differ from passive crawlers?. What patterns predict whether a page will be visible to an LLM versus a traditional search crawler?. How is the ecosystem of web-crawling bots evolving, and what does that mean for how the web gets built?. What does "machine-readable" actually mean when the machines are large language models?. These are open questions. The data to answer them exists. The researcher doesn't — yet. What You'll Do. Build the Research Infrastructure. Organize, clean, and structure Prerender's existing data for systematic analysis. Design and build new data collection streams to answer questions the current data can't. Create the foundation that makes a sustained cadence of original research possible. Conduct and Publish Original Research. Design and execute studies using Prerender's data, web-scale datasets, and primary research methods. Own the full research lifecycle: question framing, methodology, analysis, and findings. Produce work ranging from quick data snapshots to in-depth flagship studies — all published under your name. You'll build deep domain expertise along the way: profiling crawler behavior at scale by bot type, maintaining a living behavioral taxonomy of known and emerging bots (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, and whatever ships ), and tracking the frontier — bot documentation changes, LLMs.txt adoption, schema evolution, and published research. AI is both a subject and a tool in this role. You'll research how LLMs crawl, how AI search systems behave, and how autonomous agents interact with web content, while using AI tools to accelerate your own analysis, synthesis, and pattern recognition. Inform Business Decisions. Bring data to the conversations that shape what Prerender builds, how we sell, and where we invest. Work across Product, Engineering, Marketing, Sales, and leadership to replace assumptions with evidence. Who You Are. Rigorous and deeply curious — you care about getting it right and keep pulling the thread when the first answer comes too easily. Technically self-sufficient — you've built your own data pipelines, wrangled messy datasets, and written the code to get from raw data to analysis. Independent — you set your own agenda and do your best work with autonomy. A clear writer — you turn complex findings into something a non-researcher can act on. Honest — you follow the data, including when it's inconvenient. What You Bring. Required. Published original research — whether in academia or industry, you've taken a question from hypothesis through methodology to defensible conclusions. Hands-on large-scale data analysis — you've queried production data independently, not just read dashboards. Proficient in SQL and Python. Strong experiment design — hypothesis formulation, variable isolation, statistical validation, outcome measurement. Experience building data infrastructure — not just analyzing pre-built datasets. Comfort with AI tools — you're already using them in your research workflows. Nice to Have. Direct experience observing AI crawler behavior in production environments. Hands-on experience with technical SEO (crawlability, rendering, indexing, structured data). Familiarity with Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) or Generative Engine Optimization (GEO). Published or spoken publicly on machine visibility topics. Background in information retrieval or NLP. We're looking for someone whose core strength is research itself — asking good questions, designing sound methods, and following evidence to conclusions that hold up. Whether that came from a PhD program or years of applied research in industry, you're exactly who we're looking for. What's in it for You. A dataset no one else has: Billions of pages, dozens of bot types — waiting to be structured and studied. Full ownership: You define what research means here — the infrastructure, the methods, the output. Your work gets published under your name: Read, cited, and used by the industry. A real thought partner: Work directly with the CEO to shape the research agenda. A structured program, not a vague mandate: We've built a research strategy with a clear tiered output model and a six-month onboarding roadmap — you won't be guessing what success looks like. Build a reputation, not just a resume: Develop deep expertise in a growing field and a body of published work that compounds. Details. Remote: % remote. EU timezone overlap preferred. Learning budget: Conferences, courses, tools, and resources. Prerender is part of saas.group and we have a shared goal of succeeding together. What is saas.group?. Think of us as the driving catalyst behind your favorite software success stories. saas.group, established in 2017, is on a mission to turbocharge promising B2B SaaS ventures, unlocking their full potential. As a Software-as-a-Service portfolio powerhouse, we specialize in acquiring small software treasures and polishing them into industry stars. With a dynamic, fully remote team of nearly + colleagues spanning 50+ countries we are truly global and we are rewriting the playbook on SaaS innovation and collaboration. We grow 25 exciting brands under our umbrella. Feel free to explore our Candidate's Hub to get an insider's view, dive into our culture, gain valuable insights into our teams and how we work. Also, don’t forget to follow saas.group on to stay -date on our, podcast episodes, and with all things happening at our company. We don’t just simply offer you to “take a job with us” but rather to “join us on this journey” to build the world's largest platform of independent SaaS companies. Sounds like something you’re up for? Feel free to apply even if you don’t check all these boxes. We believe in the power of diversity and fresh perspectives to spice up our team. Your application might just be the missing ingredient!. —. saas.group is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. We are proud to be an. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you're passionate about working with a team that values innovation, excellence, and fairness, we encourage you to apply This role is central to that effort. You'll build the data infrastructure that makes rigorous research possible, then use it to produce work no one else can. Design and build new data collection streams to answer questions the current data can't. Bring data to the conversations that shape what Prerender builds, how we sell, and where we invest. Feel free to explore our Candidate's Hub to get an insider's view, dive into our culture, gain valuable insights into our teams and how we work

title: Campaign Manager
raw_description: This role is part of our ScraperAPI team, one of our growing brands at saas.group. ScraperAPI is an enterprise-scale web scraping solution, trusted by businesses across a wide range of industries to extract crucial data at scale. From e-commerce, travel to real estate and market research, we support companies in turning the web into actionable insights. We’re currently on an exciting growth journey, scaling rapidly as demand for reliable, high-performance web scraping and data keeps rising. Profile Overview. As our Campaign Manager, you’ll own the execution and optimization of demand campaigns across channels, from Google Ads and paid social to email, webinars, events, and our website. Reporting into the Growth Marketing Manager, you’ll be responsible for campaign builds, timelines, and delivery, working closely with Marketing Operations to ensure everything is automated, accurately tracked, and optimized for performance.This is a unique opportunity to roll up your sleeves and help build the demand engine and foundations from scratch, with space to bring ideas and make a clear stamp on what we’re building. Your immediate impact in the first 3-6 months will be:. Own -day campaign execution and optimization across paid and email, taking full operational responsibility and driving performance forward. Execute the new social media strategy from scratch, launching and iterating campaigns across key channels. Ensure performance tracking and reporting are reliable and accurate, including supporting the consolidation and smooth operation of advertising accounts. Your responsibilities. Execute demand campaigns across channels (email, paid, webinars, events, website), owning build, launch, and -day management. Own campaign timelines, coordination, and delivery in close collaboration with the Growth Marketer and the wider marketing team. Build, run, and optimize campaigns in platforms such as Google Ads, Ads, Ads, and Capterra. Create and iterate on email campaigns and sequences to support demand generation. Partner with Marketing Operations to ensure campaigns are correctly set up, automated, and tracked -end. Monitor performance daily, provide execution-level insights, proactively flag underperforming campaigns and execution risks early, and run tests/optimizations to improve results. What You bring to the table. 3+ years of hands-on experience running and optimizing campaigns across platforms such as Google Ads, Ads, Ads, Capterra, and similar tools. Experience with email marketing, including building effective email sequences. An execution-first mindset: comfortable being in the trenches daily building, launching, and optimizing campaigns with strong attention to detail. Sharp analytical thinking: able to spot patterns and performance issues quickly and act proactively (rather than reactively). Clear, structured communication skills, able to liaise upwards and keep stakeholders informed on what’s happening and what you’re seeing in the data. What’s in it for You. Ultimate flexibility: We’re % remote. You can work from wherever you like, whenever you like. Freedom and autonomy: We’re a high-trust team, and you’ll be given lots of flexibility to solve problems in your own way — with plenty of help from the team when you need it. Minimum bureaucracy: We don’t like to get bogged down with meetings and red tape. We like to be efficient and keep momentum steady & sustainable. Small & friendly team: We help each other out, have fun, and joke around. Our network: We are a community of entrepreneurial SaaS professionals that regularly exchange ideas, knowledge, learning and expertise with each other internally. Flexible time off: We want you to recharge your batteries when needed. ScraperAPI is part of saas.group and we have a shared goal of succeeding together. What is saas.group?. Think of us as the driving catalyst behind your favorite software success stories. saas.group, established in 2017, is on a mission to turbocharge promising B2B SaaS ventures, unlocking their full potential. title: Chief Executive Officer [B2B SaaS] - Talent Pool
raw_description: What is saas.group?. This job ad is intended to spark interest rather than announce an immediate opening. The role is for a future CEO position at saas.group, tied to potential upcoming acquisitions. We are continuously acquiring new businesses and are building a strong talent pool of ambitious leaders who want to step into CEO roles within our portfolio. If you see yourself leading a SaaS business with us in the future, we’d love to connect. At saas.group, we partner with ambitious leaders to transform great SaaS products into category leaders. Founded in 2017, we’ve built a global powerhouse of B2B SaaS companies by acquiring and scaling high-potential ventures. As a CEO within our group, you’ll focus on what you do best — driving growth, building high-performing teams, and shaping product strategy — while we take the weight of administration, finance, and back-office complexity off your shoulders. With the backing of a fully remote team of + colleagues across 50+ countries, you’ll run your brand with the autonomy of a founder and the support of an experienced network that sets you up for long-term success. We grow 21 exciting brands under our umbrella. Feel free to explore our Candidate's Hub to get an insider's view, dive into our culture, gain valuable insights into our teams and how we work. Also, don’t forget to follow saas.group on to stay -date on our, podcast episodes, and with all things happening at our company. Profile Overview. We are looking for a CEO to lead one of our acquisitions into its stage of growth and scale. As CEO, you will own the brand’s PnL, strategy, growth, culture, and overall performance, driving both short-term execution and long-term strategic direction. You will work closely with our leadership team, board, and the broader saas.group community to deliver impact across -market, product, operations, and finance. This role is an opportunity to shape the future of a SaaS business within a high-growth portfolio, while having the backing, network, and resources of saas.group. Your responsibilities. Define and execute a bold, market-aware brand strategy, aligning with saas.group objectives and adapting to changing dynamics.. Lead -market execution (PLG, inbound/enterprise sales, partnerships), owning commercial performance and revenue growth.. Drive operational excellence: build budgets, track KPIs, optimize ROI, and implement process improvements that increase efficiency and scalability.. Build, coach, and retain a high-performing leadership team; create succession plans and ensure organizational resilience.. Foster innovation by identifying disruptive trends (including AI), experimenting with new business models, and making data-driven bets that strengthen market position.. Champion a strong culture: set high performance standards, encourage collaboration, ensure alignment with saas.group values, and embed diversity and inclusion.. Represent the brand externally with customers, partners, investors, and industry stakeholders.. What You bring to the table. Proven leadership experience as a CEO, GM, or senior executive in a B2B SaaS company, with a strong track record of driving growth and building enduring businesses.. Demonstrated ability to craft and execute strategy, including successful GTM approaches (PLG, enterprise sales, partnerships) and strategic market decisions.. Strong financial acumen: budgeting, forecasting, and P&L responsibility, with proven ability to link financial discipline to sustainable growth.. Experience leading organizational transformation through process improvements, operational standardization, and adoption of cutting-edge practices.. A talent magnet: skilled at hiring, developing, and retaining top performers, mentoring future leaders, and creating succession pipelines.. Strong people leadership: ability to set goals, hold teams accountable, address performance issues decisively, and nurture a culture of excellence.. Visionary mindset with the ability to communicate a compelling long-term direction that inspires both internal and external stakeholders.. Excellent communication and influencing skills, with fluency in English (additional languages are a plus).. AI Literacy & Application: You actively use AI tools to enhance personal productivity and work quality, staying curious about emerging capabilities and identifying opportunities to apply AI pragmatically in -day work.. title: Content Specialist
raw_description: This role is part of our ScraperAPI team, one of our growing brands at saas.group. About ScraperAPI. ScraperAPI is an -end web data solution, trusted by businesses across a wide range of industries to extract and leverage crucial web data at scale. From e-commerce, travel to real estate and market research, we support companies in turning the web into actionable insights. We're currently on an exciting growth journey, scaling rapidly as demand for reliable, high-performance web scraping and data keeps rising. Profile Overview. We're looking for a Content Specialist to join ScraperAPI's marketing team at an exciting point in our growth. This is a hands-on, delivery-focused role where writing and running the production engine go hand in hand. You'll take the Content Lead's strategic direction and turn it into published assets that drive awareness, engagement, and demand across our channels. You'll cover a wide content surface: technical tutorials for developers, landing page copy, email sequences, ad copy, cross-channel creatives, video scripts, case studies, and more. You'll also keep the engine running by managing the content calendar, publishing across channels, coordinating freelancers or agencies, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks. This is a rare opportunity to build real ownership in a fast-moving SaaS company with a growing global customer base, as part of a broader portfolio of entrepreneurial software businesses. Your Immediate Impact in the First 3–6 Months. Start managing the content calendar and production workflow so that deadlines are met, stakeholders know what's coming, and nothing slips. Produce and distribute a consistent stream of high-quality content across technical and non-technical formats without needing heavy direction on every piece, potentially leveraging external contributors. Maintain a content performance system to evaluate and improve the content's impact on the pipeline. Your Responsibilities. Write and produce content that moves the needle: produce clear, on-brand content across blog posts, tutorials, landing pages, email copy, video scripts, webinar concepts, case studies, sales collateral, and social media, adapting tone and structure for both developer and business audiences. Manage -end content production and distribution: translate strategic direction into actionable content plans, manage the calendar, publish content, coordinate deadlines, and keep workstreams on track without needing to be chased. Coordinate external contributors: brief freelance writers, designers, and agencies; review and edit their output before publication; maintain quality and consistency across everything that goes out. Optimise for discoverability: apply SEO, AEO, and GEO principles across all content so ScraperAPI is findable wherever its audience is searching, including AI-driven channels. Use data to iterate: track content performance, report on what's working and what isn't, and feed those insights back to improve future output. Collaborate cross-functionally: work with marketing, sales, and product to ensure content reflects real customer conversations and supports commercial goals. What You Bring to the Table. 3 to 5 years of experience in content marketing or content writing in a B2B environment, ideally in SaaS, tech, or a developer-focused product. Strong writing ability across multiple formats, including blog posts, landing pages, email sequences, video scripts, social posts, case studies, and sales collateral, with the ability to write for or quickly learn to write for a technical developer audience. Proven experience managing a content calendar, publishing across channels (, X, Reddit, email), and translating marketing direction into structured content plans with clear timelines. Experience briefing and coordinating external contributors, including freelance writers, designers, or agencies, with a strong eye for quality, tone, and accuracy. Solid working knowledge of SEO and a genuine understanding of how AI-driven search (AEO/GEO) is changing content discoverability. You actively use AI tools as a core part of your content workflow. This is not a nice-to-have; it's a baseline expectation. Highly organised with strong project management instincts; you can manage multiple workstreams without being micromanaged. What's in it for You. Ultimate flexibility: We're % remote. You can work from wherever you like (within CET -2 and +2). Real ownership from day one: What you produce goes live, reaches real users, and directly impacts pipeline. You'll see the results of your work. Freedom and autonomy: We're a high-trust team, and you'll be given lots of flexibility to solve problems in your own way, with plenty of help from the team when you need it. Minimum bureaucracy: We don't like to get bogged down with meetings and red tape. We like to be efficient and keep momentum steady and sustainable. Small and friendly team: We help each other out, have fun, and joke around. Our network: We are a community of entrepreneurial SaaS professionals that regularly exchange ideas, knowledge, learning and expertise with each other internally. Flexible time off: We want you to recharge your batteries when needed. Room to grow: The role is designed to evolve toward owning full campaigns independently, with a clear path toward Content Manager. title: General Counsel & Head of Legal Operations (AI-First)
raw_description: The Mission. We are a Delaware-incorporated, remote-first serial acquirer of SaaS companies across North America and Europe. With a portfolio spanning 6+ jurisdictions, a $100M debt facility, and a -person global team, our complexity is high, but our team is lean. We are looking for our first General Counsel & Head of Legal Ops. We don't want to build a massive legal department; we want to build a massive legal advantage. You will be the architect of an AI-augmented legal and tax function that enables us to acquire 5+ companies per year with unprecedented speed and structural efficiency. The "AI-First" Mandate. In this role, your "Junior Associate" and "Tax Senior" are Large Language Models (LLMs). You will:. Scale through Automation: Implement AI-driven due diligence to audit hundreds of SaaS contracts in hours. AI-Augmented Tax Structuring: Use advanced AI agents to perform "first-pass" research on cross-border tax treaties (US/EU), transfer pricing logic, and permanent establishment risks. Self-Serve Governance: Deploy internal AI tools to handle 80% of routine queries from our global team regarding SARs, HR compliance, and NDAs. Outside Counsel Optimization: Act as a "sophisticated buyer" of legal and tax services—using AI to verify work and ensuring we only pay for high-level "human-in-the-loop" sign-offs. Key Responsibilities. M&A Execution: Lead the legal lifecycle of ~5 acquisitions/year. Own the SPA, manage local counsel in Europe/NA, and drive deals to close. Strategic Tax Structuring: Work at the intersection of legal and finance. Use AI to model tax-efficient deal structures (Asset vs. Share) and maintain our global transfer pricing framework between Delaware and our European subsidiaries. Debt & Capital: Manage our $100M debt facility—handling drawdowns, ensuring covenant compliance, and managing lender relations. Global People Ops: Oversee compliance for a -person workforce (FTEs, Contractors, EOR) across Germany, Austria, France, Finland, Canada, and the US. Equity (SARs): Manage and scale our Stock Appreciation Rights program globally. Who You Are. The M&A Quarterback: You have 6–10 years of experience (JD/LLM required), likely coming from a top-tier Private Equity or M&A practice. AI-Native: You don't just "use" AI; you prompt-engineer your workflow. You are comfortable building a "no-code" legal stack. The "Tax-Literate" Generalist: You aren't a CPA, but you understand the "geometry" of international tax. You can use AI to navigate a tax treaty and know exactly when to pull in a specialist for a final "stress test.". Operational Mindset: You value deal velocity over long memos. You quantify risk in dollars, not just "could/might" language. The Tech Stack You’ll Build. We expect you to own the budget for and implement:. AI Due Diligence: For contract analysis and liability mapping. Entity Management: For our 6+ global subsidiaries. CLM (Contract Lifecycle Management): To automate SaaS sales and vendor agreements. Tax AI Research: To stay ahead of OECD/Pillar Two and local European tax changes. Why Join Us?. Blueprint Power: You are the first hire. You define the tech stack and the strategy. title: Operations Data Analyst
raw_description: Benvenuti nel futuro dell’energia! La missione di Octopus Energy è rendere l’energia rinnovabile accessibile a tutti. Grazie alla tecnologia, stiamo guidando la rivoluzione energetica a livello globale, offrendo energia pulita a prezzi convenienti e un servizio clienti eccellente. Se non ti piacciono le sfide non inviarci la tua candidatura;) In Octopus Energy non amiamo la burocrazia, le regole troppo stringenti e le offerte commerciali poco chiare. Vogliamo rivoluzionare il mercato energetico italiano. Per farlo, siamo alla ricerca di persone motivate, che condividano i nostri stessi valori. Se hai a cuore il futuro del nostro pianeta e vuoi aiutarci a migliorare il settore energetico, unisciti al nostro team! Con la crescita vertiginosa che stiamo avendo in Octopus, è emersa la straordinaria opportunità di fare la differenza andando a utilizzare in modo intelligente e creativo la grande mole di dati che ogni giorno gestiamo come compagnia energetica. title: Dual Fuel Smart Meter Engineer (London)
raw_description: Help us make a big green dent in the universe. We're on a mission to drive the global renewable energy revolution and need your help to turn our vision into a reality. We're looking for smart meter engineers with a track record of safety and customer excellence. You’ll be representing our brands in our customers’ homes so you’ll need to be just as happy talking to the customer and getting to know them as you are about doing the installation itself. You’ll be fitting meters; showing customers how their meter and in-home display work; how it can help them save energy and answering any questions about the green energy revolution we are leading. As part of the Octopus Energy Services team, we’ll ensure you have the best of everything you need to deliver the top-class service we’re aiming for. You’ll be supported by an office team who will share the same performance goals, so we’re always working as one team. title: Dual Fuel Smart Meter Engineer (East Midlands)
raw_description: Help us make a big green dent in the universe. We're on a mission to drive the global renewable energy revolution and need your help to turn our vision into a reality. We're looking for smart meter engineers with a track record of safety and customer excellence. You’ll be representing our brands in our customers’ homes so you’ll need to be just as happy talking to the customer and getting to know them as you are about doing the installation itself. You’ll be fitting meters; showing customers how their meter and in-home display work; how it can help them save energy and answering any questions about the green energy revolution we are leading. As part of the Octopus Energy Services team, we’ll ensure you have the best of everything you need to deliver the top-class service we’re aiming for. You’ll be supported by an office team who will share the same performance goals, so we’re always working as one team. title: Backend Developer Python
raw_description: Octopus Energy, c’est l’histoire d’une idée audacieuse: rendre l’énergie plus verte, moins chère et accessible à tous, en créant le premier fournisseur d'énergie véritablement digital. Et si le projet a d’abord émergé au Royaume-Uni, depuis 2022, ce projet s’étend aussi à la France! 🇫🇷 En associant une technologie de pointe à des personnes talentueuses, profondément attachées autant à nos clients qu’à la transition vers les énergies renouvelables, nous continuons de concrétiser ce projet. En trois ans, nous sommes ainsi devenus le cinquième fournisseur français! Notre mission? Fournir une énergie moins chère et plus verte à tous les Français, ainsi que des solutions concrètes pour accélérer la transition écologique. Aujourd'hui, nous développons la prochaine génération de produits qui accélèrent la transition vers les énergies vertes. Qu'il s'agisse de faciliter le passage à l'énergie

title: Affiliate Marketing Associate
raw_description: With the most comprehensive collection of trails in the world, AllTrails supports inclusive access to nature for a global community of millions of trailgoers. While this role does not require in-person attendance, we have a preference for candidates based in or near one of these cities: San Francisco CA, Portland OR, Seattle WA, Denver CO, and New York NY. The AllTrails marketing team is building a global, beloved outdoors brand. The Affiliate Marketing Associate is on the Brand Engagement team and reports to the Senior Lead, Affiliate Partnerships. This role is responsible for owning the execution of AllTrails’ global affiliate program while contributing strategic insights based on partner performance and market trends

title: Lead Urban Designer
raw_description: Description Ascent is looking for a Lead Urban Designer to join our Urban Design + Planning Studio. Our ideal candidate is a seasoned urban designer with deep California experience, a collaborative attitude, and a track record of leading specific plans, master plans, community plans, zoning, form-based codes, design standards, feasibility studies, and community engagement. This is a leadership role within our Urban Design + Planning Studio. In a typical week, you might: - Drive the design vision – Lead specific plans, master plans, and other urban design and planning projects from concept to delivery, including visioning, site analysis, feasibility studies, and design concepts and strategies. - Develop compelling visuals – Develop 2D and 3D plans and graphics, massing studies, site plans, concept diagrams, 3D renderings, and before/after simulations that bring ideas to life

title: Mid-Level Urban Planner
raw_description: Description Our Urban Design + Planning Studio is looking for a mid-level Urban Planner to join our team in either Sacramento or San Diego. Our ideal candidate has experience working on long-range planning projects in California, including general plans, housing elements, specific plans, zoning, and community engagement. In a typical week, you might: - Lead and support planning projects – Contribute to the preparation of housing elements, general plans, specific plans, and zoning codes. Why Ascent We're an Urban Design + Planning studio that believes planning is one of the most powerful tools we have to advance housing equity and build more livable, inclusive communities – and we bring that passion to everything we do. To ensure candidates are paid fairly, the offered salary will fall within this range and will be determined based on the candidate's demonstrated and alignment with the of the position outlined above

title: Deputy Director, Non-Incentive Programs
raw_description: Please be aware of recruiting scams! All legitimate communication from our recruitment team will come from an official calstart.org email address via email, we will not text you about a role you have not applied to or shown interest in. We will not perform any interviews via text or Zoom chat. CALSTART does not ask for any fees or personal information such as social security numbers or bank details during the recruitment process. About Us: CALSTART is a mission-driven industry organization focused on transportation decarbonization and clean air for all. For over 30 years, it’s been CALSTART’s mission to develop, assess, and implement large-scale, zero-emission transportation solutions to mitigate climate change and support economic growth. CALSTART works with businesses, organizations, governments, and communities to create real-life impact toward clean air and equitable access to clean transportation for all. CALSTART provides scientific, technical and policy support for regulatory development and clean technology and infrastructure acceleration. About The Role: ​​The Deputy Director, Non-Incentive Programs plays a key leadership role within CALSTART’s Clean Fuels & Infrastructure team, driving the execution and delivery of high-impact, non-incentive zero-emission transportation initiatives. Reporting to the Senior Director, Clean Fuels & Infrastructure, this role translates portfolio strategy into actionable workplans, oversees complex projects from planning through delivery, and ensures consistent, high-quality outcomes across grid integration, utility engagement, market development, and infrastructure initiatives. The Deputy Director serves as a central integrator across technical, policy, and market-facing teams while maintaining a strong focus on execution, accountability, and continuous improvement. This is an ideal opportunity for a collaborative, execution-focused leader who enjoys managing people and programs while shaping the future of clean transportation infrastructure. The role supervises project managers and technical staff, supports professional development, and contributes to business development and strategic growth of CALSTART’s non-incentive portfolio. With meaningful responsibility, visibility, and impact, the Deputy Director helps advance CALSTART’s mission by ensuring projects are delivered on time, on budget, and in close partnership with members, utilities, and stakeholders working to accelerate the transition to zero-emission transportation.​ What You'll Do: Lead execution of non-incentive clean transportation projects, overseeing -day delivery of complex zero-emission transportation initiatives across grid integration, utility engagement, market development, and infrastructure planning, while managing scopes, schedules, budgets, risks, and dependencies. Supervise and develop staff (3-4 CALSTARs), including project managers and technical team members, by providing coaching, performance feedback, and professional development support aligned with CALSTART’s people-management and inclusive leadership practices. Translate portfolio strategy into action, working closely with the Senior Director to convert organizational priorities into executable project plans, workplans, and roadmaps that deliver measurable outcomes. Serve as a cross-functional integrator, coordinating closely with policy, other CALSTART initiatives especially Light-duty, Innovative Mobility, Truck and Off Road and Drive to Zero, as well as non-initiative teams like Data and Analytics, Demonstration and Technical Assistance, Member Engagement, and Business Development teams to ensure alignment, information flow, and high-quality delivery across the non-incentive portfolio. Ensure delivery excellence and continuous improvement, tracking milestones, KPIs, and outcomes; proactively identifying risks; and implementing corrective actions and process improvements to strengthen project transparency, reporting, and performance. Support utility and grid-focused initiatives, corridor-planning strategies and infrastructure technical offerings contributing to zero-emission transportation infrastructure planning, corridor development, and innovative efforts that advance CALSTART’s mission. Engage external stakeholders and partners, representing CALSTART in meetings with members, utilities, and partners, and contributing credible, responsive leadership in program and project discussions. Contribute to organizational growth, supporting non-incentive business development through business and proposal development, project scoping, partnership coordination, and advancement of new opportunities.​ What You'll Bring To The Table: 7–10 years of progressive professional experience in clean transportation, energy, utilities, infrastructure development, or closely related fields. Bachelor’s degree required in a relevant field such as: business management, engineering, environmental science, public policy, energy, transportation, or a related discipline. PMP preferred but not required. Demonstrated success managing complex projects or programs involving multiple stakeholders, timelines, and deliverables. Direct experience with non-incentive zero-emission transportation initiatives, including infrastructure planning, utility engagement, grid-related projects, or corridor development for light-, medium-, and heavy-duty vehicles and hydrogen infrastructure. Formal people-management or supervisory experience, including coaching, performance management, and staff development. Experience working cross-functionally with technical, policy, market development, and member-facing teams. Strong project and program management skills, with the ability to manage scopes, schedules, budgets, risks, and dependencies. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to convey complex information clearly to internal and external stakeholders. Proven stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, particularly with utilities, partners, and members. Sound judgment and decision-making skills, with the ability to provide analysis and recommendations to senior leadership. Familiarity with project management tools and methodologies (PMP certification preferred). Experience tracking milestones, KPIs, and outcomes using project management, reporting, or collaboration software (Monday.com, Mavenlink, ADP). Competency with common productivity and collaboration platforms (e.g., document sharing, project tracking, presentation tools). Execution & Delivery: Consistently delivers high-quality work on time and within scope. People Leadership: Applies inclusive leadership practices and supports team growth and development. Collaboration: Builds trust and alignment across diverse internal teams and external partners. Continuous Improvement: Identifies opportunities to strengthen processes, transparency, and performance. Accountability: Takes ownership of responsibilities and outcomes at both the individual and team level.​ Desired Skills: Master's degree in engineering, energy systems, transportation planning, environmental policy, public administration, business, organizational development, or a related field Formal training or certifications in project management, leadership development, systems thinking, or change management beyond core requirements Experience leading or supporting large-scale, multi-jurisdictional infrastructure or market transformation initiatives Prior involvement with utility planning processes (especially distribution), regulatory proceedings, or grid modernization efforts related to transportation electrification or hydrogen infrastructure Experience contributing to or leading proposal development, partnership strategy, or business development in a consulting, nonprofit, or public-sector setting Familiarity with national, state, or regional clean transportation policies and emerging market trends Comfort working in matrixed organizations and navigating ambiguity in fast-evolving technical, regulatory, or market environments Experience facilitating workshops, stakeholder convenings, or collaborative planning processes Demonstrated ability to mentor emerging leaders and support succession planning within teams Strong systems-level thinking and the ability to connect strategic goals to operational execution Adaptive leadership style with a continuous-improvement mindset and openness to evolving best practices High EQ, diplomacy, and ability to build trust across diverse internal teams and external partners Ability to participate in occasional travel (approximately 10-20%), including attending meetings, site visits, and conferences Ability to adjust work schedules as needed to meet with other teams in other time zones (nationally and internationally) our recruitment team will come from an official calstart. About The Role: We are seeking a skilled Business Analyst for a full-time, 6-month (with the opportunity to extend) on a contract basis. ​​The Technical Business Analyst plays a critical role in translating complex program policies and business needs into clear, testable software requirements for multiple program teams using Salesforce and related tools. This role is focused on requirements quality and SDLC execution, ensuring that work entering delivery is well-defined, measurable, and fully “interlocked” into development and testing practices. The Technical Business Analyst partners closely with Program teams, Project/Program Managers, Salesforce Admins, Developers (internal and vendor), and QA/UAT stakeholders to drive clarity, reduce rework, and support consistent delivery outcomes across the lifecycle from intake through release. This position is critical in fast-moving environments where multiple stakeholders (internal teams, external partners, and developers) are contributing feedback simultaneously. The Technical Business Analyst protects delivery momentum by creating structure, clarity, and a single source of truth across UAT, backlog management, and solution alignment. ​ CALSTART values transparency and strives to provide as much information regarding compensation as possible. The rate for this contract position is $50 per hour. title: Power Systems Engineer
raw_description: Join us on the journey to get to net zero. At Carbon Direct, we dedicate our scientific, software, and business expertise to empower organizations around the world to take climate action.. Our Mission. Enable organizations to reduce, remove, and utilize their emissions with carbon science. We are a purpose-driven carbon management firm dedicated to helping organizations around the world reach their climate goals. We make carbon science accessible and actionable with our -end platform.. Global citizens with global impact. Whether a scientist, developer, or carbon markets expert, we are united by our mission to take climate action now. We are experts in our fields and we act with confidence. Located across 4 countries and in states all across the U.S., we offer both remote-friendly work options and dynamic, in-person experiences with offices located in Seattle, WA, Oakland, CA, and NYC.. Diverse backgrounds bring diverse perspectives. We recognize that teams with diverse backgrounds and different experiences are powerful. Bringing together a variety of perspectives only enhances how we can effectively address the climate crisis. Together, we are creating an environment where everyone is celebrated and anyone can succeed. The Role. Overview. We are seeking an Electrical Engineer to join our growing Data and Innovation team. This role sits at the intersection of power systems engineering, energy economics, and applied machine learning to support Carbon Direct's mission of accelerating science-based carbon management. You will work with the team of engineers, climate technology experts, and applied researchers: contributing to client engagements, internal analyses, and the development of best practices across our decarbonization work. Responsibilities. Develop and operationalize ML/AI models to support real-time and forward-looking models, including load, generation, price forecasting and renewable (wind/solar) generation forecasting. Design, evaluate, and analyze electrical systems relevant to decarbonization pathways, including electrification, grid integration, power electronics, and low-carbon energy technologies. Contribute to long-range transmission and integrated resource planning, including renewable integration assessments and production cost modeling. Conduct rigorous technical due diligence on projects, technologies, and proposals involving electrical infrastructure, energy storage, or industrial electrification. Translate complex engineering analyses into clear, actionable recommendations for clients, internal teams, and executive stakeholders. Collaborate cross-functionally with scientists, policy experts, and business development teams to support client deliverables and thought leadership. Contribute to publications, technical reports, and external communications related to electrification and grid decarbonization. Stay current on developments in power systems modeling, climate technology, grid modernization, and industrial decarbonization. Qualifications. Required:. Master's or Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering or a related quantitative field. Expertise in the electric power industry, with exposure to wholesale electricity markets and/or grid operations. Proficiency in AC/DC power flow models, experienced in working with production-cost models such as PROMOD, Plexos, AURORA. Proficiency in statistical modeling, forecasting, and data analysis (Python, R, MATLAB, or equivalent). Deep familiarity with energy market structures (ISOs/RTOs), capacity markets, resource adequacy frameworks, and reliability standards. Experience deploying machine learning models in production environments. Experience working across multidisciplinary teams, strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present complex analytics to non-technical stakeholders. Preferred:. Experience modeling energy storage systems, EV infrastructure, building electrification, or industrial decarbonization. Familiarity with GHG accounting, energy policy, or carbon market frameworks. Prior experience in consulting, policy, or a climate-focused organization working with corporate clients. Professional licensure (PE) or advanced credentials in relevant electrical engineering domains. Published or public-facing technical work in electrification, grid modernization, or related areas. Knowledge and experience with ISO/RTO operations. Familiarity with FERC regulatory proceedings and filings and power market design. Knowledge of natural gas/power market dynamics. Salary Range. $133,000-$195,000 a year. Benefits. While we love our work, our lives aren’t defined by it. At Carbon Direct, we offer a robust benefits package that considers the whole person, their partners, and their families – and we’re always looking to improve.. Our U.S. benefits* include:. Comprehensive nationwide medical, dental, and vision coverage. See a preview of our coverage here. Time off as needed: Flexible vacation policy and ten company-wide holidays, plus annual winter break between Christmas and New Year's. 16 weeks of fully paid parental and family leave with no tenure requirement. Remote-friendly work culture with annual company-wide retreats. Reimbursement for your work-from-home setup and monthly work-from-home stipend. *Please note that the benefits described apply to U.S.-based employees only. For our international employees, our benefits package varies by each country and its statutory requirements... Carbon Direct is an. We adhere rigorously to our equal employment opportunity policies in connection with all employment decisions, including hiring, compensation and promotion We recognize that teams with diverse backgrounds and different experiences are powerful. At Carbon Direct, we offer a robust package that considers the whole person, their partners, and their families – and we’re always looking to improve. Together, we are creating an environment where everyone is celebrated and anyone can succeed. Published or public-facing technical work in electrification, grid modernization, or related areas

title: Digital Program Manager
raw_description: We are seeking an experienced and motivated individual to join Vocal Media's team! As a Digital Program Manager, you'll oversee -day operations of client social media programs, managing a dedicated team of social media managers while ensuring seamless program execution and client satisfaction. You'll serve as the primary operational lead for client accounts, balancing staff management, program logistics, client communication, and strategic guidance. title: Meme Creator
raw_description: We’re looking for meme creators who have their finger on the pulse of the internet — tracking, interpreting, and predicting trends before they hit the mainstream. These contract roles focus on developing and executing vertical video-first content strategies across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and other social platforms. The ideal candidate brings deep platform fluency, sharp trend awareness, and the ability to rapidly create compelling, shareable content on topics like, finance, and culture in a fast-paced, ever-evolving digital landscape. Responsibilities Time Commitment & Output Dedicate 10-12 hours weekly with flexible scheduling; Participate in two 30-min weekly check-ins about the project; Produce and manage 10-15 posts per account for 2+ accounts weekly across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and platforms. Strategy & Analysis Monitor emerging platform trends and algorithm updates to continuously refine content approach; Track and analyze key performance metrics and audience engagement patterns. Collaboration Work effectively with fellow team members and Content leadership; Contribute to cross-platform content initiatives and strategy discussions. title: Investor Engagement Manager
raw_description: In this position, you will have the opportunity to lead and grow GFI’s efforts to champion the inclusion of alternative proteins (APs) in investment portfolios, identifying opportunities and solutions to mobilize capital for alternative protein scale-up and commercialization. While GFI’s work does not deploy capital directly to companies, this position will focus on shaping investment ecosystems, informing capital allocation decisions, engaging with investors, and accelerating capital flows through analysis, education, and strategic engagement. GFI’s Investor Engagement Manager will be responsible for:. Developing pathways for derisking investment in alternative proteins, including securing partnerships to explore novel capital solutions to support scale-up and commercialization and convening capital providers around specific bottlenecks. Collaborating across GFI and with external partners to analyze and expand the public investment landscape for alternative proteins

title: Senior Software Engineer, AI Platform
raw_description: About GoodLeap: GoodLeap is a technology company delivering best-in-class financing and software products for sustainable solutions, from solar panels and batteries to energy-efficient HVAC, heat pumps, roofing, windows, and more. Over 1 million homeowners have benefited from our simple, fast, and frictionless technology that makes the adoption of these products more affordable, accessible, and easier to understand. Thousands of professionals deploying home efficiency and solar solutions rely on GoodLeap’s proprietary, AI-powered applications and developer tools to drive more transparent customer communication, deeper business intelligence, and streamlined payment and operations. Our platform has led to more than $30 billion in financing for sustainable solutions since 2018. GoodLeap is also proud to support our award-winning nonprofit, GivePower, which is building and deploying life-saving water and clean electricity systems, changing the lives of more than 1.6 million people across Africa, Asia, and South America. Position Summary Build features and extensions to our agentic AI platform using scalable, robust, and AI-first software engineering practices. Design tools and infrastructure to enable teams at GoodLeap to easily build and enhance AI agents that empower homeowners, contractors, and operations staff. title: Senior Director, Climate and Rights (12-month position)
raw_description: ABOUT GREENPEACE. Greenpeace is a global, independent campaigning organization that uses peaceful protest, creative communication and people power to expose global environmental problems and promote solutions that are essential to a green and peaceful future. In just the past year, we’ve secured a Global Oceans Treaty to protect wildlife and people who depend on healthy oceans. We persuaded the Biden Administration to phase out single-use plastics from federal operations. And we safeguarded healthy spaces, from national marine sanctuaries to bans on seabed mining to stopping liquefied national gas terminals poisoning Gulf Coast communities. Our work spans more than 50 years of fights and successes. As an independent campaigning organization, Greenpeace does not accept funds from any corporations or government entities. The global Greenpeace network is headquartered in the Netherlands, with offices around the world in over fifty countries. Greenpeace USA is headquartered in Washington, DC.. POSITION SUMMARY. The United States is at a historic crossroads. Climate impacts are accelerating, democratic institutions and civic rights are under sustained attack, and corporate power continues to undermine both environmental progress and people’s ability to shape the future. The decisions made in this moment will reverberate for generations. Greenpeace USA seeks a dynamic, strategic, and values-driven leader to serve as Senior Director, Climate & Rights —a senior role responsible for leading and integrating our climate and democracy campaign portfolios as a unified, coordinated body of work. This role recognizes a core truth of our time: a livable climate depends on a functioning democracy, protected civic space, and the right to dissent. The Senior Director, Climate & Rights is a highly visible leadership role within the Programs Department, and will collaborate in close partnership with the Senior Oceans Campaign Director and other senior program leaders across GPUS's Program, Legal, Communications, Engagement and Development teams. This position will also manage two direct reports - the Democracy Campaign Director and the Climate Campaign Director. The Senior Director, Climate & Rights is a highly visible leadership role within the Programs Department and reports to the Chief Program Officer. The Director manages the Climate Campaign Director and Democracy Campaign Director, and works in close partnership with the Senior Director of Oceans and other senior program leaders. The Senior Director, Climate & Rights is a 12-month contract full-time, exempt, benefits-eligible, hybrid, position, preferably based in Washington D.C. Candidates in other GPUS-authorized locations (CA, NY, NJ, MA) will be considered. This role reports to the Chief Program Officer. RESPONSIBILITIES. Strategic Leadership and Integration. Provide senior leadership and strategic direction for Greenpeace USA’s integrated Climate & Rights portfolio, in partnership with the Chief Program Officer and Executive Director. Ensure that climate and democracy strategies are aligned, coordinated, and mutually reinforcing, with clear priorities and outcomes. Translate organizational strategy, risk appetite, and capacity constraints into focused, executable campaign direction. Identify opportunities where democracy and rights strategies strengthen climate outcomes—and vice versa. Provide leadership for our work to defend speech and peaceful protest against SLAPP lawsuits. Partner with Development and senior leadership to support fundraising efforts, helping translate Climate & Rights strategies and impact into compelling donor narratives, funder briefings, and strategic opportunities that advance Greenpeace USA’s revenue and growth goals. Management of Senior Campaign Leaders. Directly manage and support the Climate Campaign Director and Democracy Campaign Director, serving as a coach, thought partner, and accountability point. Ensure Directors and their teams are aligned on strategy, timelines, resourcing, and ways of working. Support Directors in navigating complexity, tradeoffs, and decision-making in a high-risk and fast-moving environment. Foster a culture of collaboration, learning, and shared ownership across the Climate & Rights portfolio. Ensure best practices are applied to budget management, reporting requirements and other internal processes. Campaign Cohesion &Organizational Alignment. Ensure campaign strategies are developed with strong integration across legal, communications, engagement, fundraising, and operations. Partner closely with the Senior Director of Oceans to ensure alignment across program priorities, shared narratives, and organizational sequencing. Work with program leadership to assess tradeoffs, sequencing, and resourcing across issue areas. Support consistent evaluation, learning, and iteration across campaigns. People-Centered Leadership and Culture. Model and reinforce Greenpeace USA’s values of justice, nonviolence, and people-powered change. Support healthy, sustainable workloads and clear decision-making structures across teams. Foster transparent, consistent, and fair management practices that support trust, accountability, and effective collaboration across teams. Champion inclusive leadership and equitable outcomes in both internal operations and external campaign work. External Representation & Movement Leadership. Represent Greenpeace USA externally, as appropriate, with partners, allies, funders, and movement leaders. In partnership with the Chief Program Officer and issue Campaign Directors, serve as a key connector to program and campaign leaders across the global Greenpeace network, supporting alignment between U.S. climate and rights priorities and broader organizational efforts. Maintain awareness of political, legal, and movement trends related to climate, democracy, civic space, and rights. Support Directors in cultivating strong relationships with allies, coalitions, and funders. Other Duties. Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Other duties and responsibilities may be assigned as organizational or team priorities arise and/or shift. ROLE REQUIREMENTS. Knowledge and Experience:. 10+ years of senior leadership experience in climate, democracy, human rights, or movement-based organizations. Demonstrated experience managing senior leaders or directors and leading complex, multi-issue portfolios. Experience working across functions (campaigns, legal, communications, engagement, development). Proven ability to lead collaboratively, manage risk, and operate effectively amid uncertainty. In-depth knowledge of US politics and culture, social movements and socio-economic environment. Experience developing and managing campaign budgets and high priority projects and campaigns. Strong strategic campaigning experience using varied approaches, including a strong commitment to non-violent direct action as a means of affecting change. Proven track record in public/supporter engagement, mobilization and organizing, including familiarity with tools and systems to bring supporters into the work of the organization. Experience integrating climate and democracy or rights-based strategies, preferred. Familiarity with nonprofit governance, unionized workplaces, and global federated organizations, preferred. Experience working in coalition with grassroots, frontline, and movement partners, preferred. Skills/Attributes/Competencies;. Strategic integration and systems thinking. Strong strategic judgment with the ability to prioritize and make tradeoffs in constrained environments. Senior-level people management and coaching. Clear decision-making and prioritization. Strong communication skills, including serving as an external spokesperson. Political judgment and risk awareness. Strong political analysis of corporate power, civic repression, and climate justice, preferred. Collaborative leadership and influence. Commitment to equity, justice, and people power. Deep commitment to social, racial, and environmental justice and to Greenpeace USA’s mission and values. Other:. Ability and willingness to travel domestically and internationally, sometimes for significant lengths of time, as needed. Ability and willingness to work across time zones and frequently outside of normal business hours, as needed. COMPENSATION. Greenpeace offers a competitive benefits package including medical, dental and vision coverage with low contributions from staff. We have options for pre-tax flexible spending accounts for medical and dependent care costs. Greenpeace employees enjoy generous 401K matching and time-off for vacation, sick, personal, and parental leave. After five years, staff are eligible for a paid sabbatical. To encourage public transportation use and biking, we offer subsidies for staff who choose to bike or ride mass transit to work. Greenpeace is committed to the growth and development of staff and fostering a creative workplace culture. For more information, check out our benefits plan. This role is a graded at a level 6, and a starting salary within the range of $144,000 to $165,600 is anticipated to be provided to the successful candidate having met the minimum requirements for this position consistent with reflecting our commitment to maintaining competitive and fair compensation practices.. If you want to save the world from climate change and believe in environmental and social justice, please submit an application on our careers page. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis and will conclude once we have found the best candidate. Greenpeace is an. We strongly encourage applications from marginalized communities. If you require assistance applying to this opportunity, please contact usa-resumes@greenpeace.org. title: Senior Manager, Marketplace & Retail Channels
raw_description: Grove Collaborative is a sustainability-focused consumer products company creating household and personal care essentials that are effective, beautifully designed, and healthier for people and the planet. We are a certified B Corp, plastic-neutral, and on a mission to transform the CPG industry for good.. About the Role. We’re looking for an experienced marketplace operator to help scale our portfolio of owned brands across Amazon, TikTok Shop, QVC, and iHerb. Amazon is a growth driver, and this role is focused on driving performance and execution across channels, with strong ownership of how work gets done -day. You’ll partner closely with the VP of Merchandising, our Amazon agency, QVC broker, and cross-functional teams to translate business priorities into clear plans, disciplined execution, and measurable results. This is a Senior Manager role—you’ll operate with a high degree of independence, lead complex workstreams, and influence outcomes across teams, while not owning the full business segment strategy. You will also have a small internal team reporting to you. You’ll need to be someone who can navigate ambiguity, make strong tactical decisions, and keep the business moving forward when things aren’t clearly defined. What You’ll Own. Channel Strategy & Execution. Support and execute channel strategy across Amazon, TikTok Shop, QVC, and iHerb. Drive performance against:. Revenue targets. Contribution profit goals. Inventory and margin guardrails. Translate high-level priorities into channel-level action plans. Amazon Growth Execution. Lead execution of Amazon strategy across owned brands, including:. Assortment. Pricing and promotions. Content and merchandising. Subscribe & Save. Partner with agency to ensure priorities are clearly defined and delivered. Agency & Broker Management. Manage Amazon agency and QVC broker relationships. Set expectations, track deliverables, and ensure follow-through. Escalate issues and adjust direction when performance or priorities shift. Cross-Functional Coordination. Partner closely with:. Finance. Inventory / Supply Chain. Owned Brands teams. Align execution with:. Inventory constraints. Financial targets. Brand priorities. Forecasting & Operational Planning. Support channel forecasting and inventory planning. Ensure plans align with operational realities and business goals. Flag risks early and recommend adjustments. How You’ll Show Up. At Grove, this role is about being a strong, independent operator who can manage complexity, influence across teams, and drive execution in evolving conditions. What We’re Looking For. Required. 7+ years in ecommerce or marketplace roles with direct Amazon ownership. Hands-on experience with Seller Central or Vendor Central. Experience managing external agencies and holding them accountable. Strong analytical skills (Excel / Google Sheets). Experience working across multi-brand or multi-SKU portfolios. Strong cross-functional collaboration skills. Hands-on operator mindset. Nice to Have. TikTok Shop experience. QVC / HSN or live commerce exposure. iHerb or specialty retail experience. Consumer products or CPG background. Brand management exposure. Experience working with inventory planning. What This Role Is (and Isn’t). You will:. Drive execution and influence outcomes across multiple channels. Translate strategy into action. You will not:. Own full P&L strategy at the enterprise level. Define company-wide direction. What Success Looks Like in Year One. Amazon delivers strong growth within defined performance guardrails. Execution across channels is consistent, disciplined, and less reactive. Agency and broker relationships are structured and accountable. Cross-functional partners have clear visibility into priorities and tradeoffs. Marketplace operations run with improved clarity, cadence, and accountability. What’s In It for You. This full-time, exempt position is remote for candidates based in the following states: California, Maine, Pennsylvania, Nevada, North Carolina, Texas, Colorado, Washington, Illinois, New York, Missouri, and Massachusetts. Competitive benefits - medical, vision, dental. Equity - shared success is core to our mission. Flexible Paid Time Off - we care most about results. Free VIP membership and 25% employee discount. Working for a company that believes that a small group of people can change the world for the better by creating products and funding initiatives that help the planet!. Compensation. The salary range for this role is $125,000-156,000. Our compensation bands are determined based on market-specific cost of living data, meaning that the top of our salary range is reserved for the most experienced candidates in the highest cost of living areas across the country. In addition to the base salary, this role is eligible for an annual incentive target and equity. MORE ABOUT GROVE. Grove Collaborative Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: GROV) is the one-stop online destination for everyday essentials that create a healthier home and planet. Explore thousands of thoughtfully vetted products for every room and everyone in your home, including household cleaning, personal care, health and wellness, laundry, clean beauty, kitchen, pantry, kids, baby, pet care, and beyond. Everything Grove sells meets a higher standard — from health to sustainability and performance — so you get a great value without compromising your values. As a B Corp and Public Benefit Corporation, Grove goes beyond selling products: every order is carbon neutral, supports plastic waste cleanup initiatives, and lets you see and track the positive impact of your choices. title: Strategic Finance Manager, Temp
raw_description: Grove Collaborative is a sustainability-focused consumer products company creating household and personal care essentials that are effective, beautifully designed, and healthier for people and the planet. We are a certified B Corp, plastic-neutral, and on a mission to transform the CPG industry for good.. THE ROLE. We’re looking for a Strategic Finance Manager to help us understand what’s happening in the business today—and what’s coming. This is a senior individual contributor role focused on financial modeling, forecasting, and business insight. You’ll work across Marketing, Fulfillment, and Leadership to translate data into decisions—helping Grove operate more efficiently and scale more intentionally. You’ll own complex analyses, build models from scratch, and partner closely with stakeholders to guide decisions. This is a role for someone who is both analytically deep and highly curious—able to navigate ambiguity and deliver clarity. This role is a 6 month temp commitment (but it's possible we'll need you permanently!). YOUR IMPACT. Financial Modeling & Forecasting. Support Grove’s operating forecast (weekly/monthly). Build and maintain models across:. LTV / CAC by channel. Marketing efficiency and spend allocation. Fulfillment and operational metrics. Identify risks, pressure-test assumptions, and proactively adjust outlook. Marketing & Growth Analytics. Analyze paid media performance. Evaluate customer acquisition efficiency and cohort behavior. Partner with Marketing to optimize spend and improve paybacks. Fulfillment & Cost Structure Analysis. Model shipping, labor, and operational costs. Identify cost drivers and opportunities for margin improvement. Support international expansion. Executive Support. Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights. Support leadership and board-level discussions as needed. Operate confidently in high-visibility environments. HOW YOU'LL SHOW UP. At Grove, this role is about operating as a senior, high-impact individual contributor—someone who can work through complex business problems and deliver practical, decision-ready insights. Own complex analytical problems: You’ll work on high-impact questions where the right answer requires evaluating multiple variables and tradeoffs. Apply strong judgment: You’ll determine the best approach to modeling and analysis, adapting methods as needed to get to clear outcomes. Connect the dots across the business: You understand how Marketing, Fulfillment, and Finance interrelate—and reflect that in your work. Operate with autonomy: You’re trusted to own your work -end, with output reviewed for alignment to broader goals rather than step-by-step direction. Influence through insight: You’ll regularly partner with stakeholders across teams, using data and clear communication to guide decisions. Balance rigor and pragmatism: You know when to go deep—and when to move forward with imperfect data to keep the business moving. WHAT THIS ROLE IS (and isn't). This is a senior IC role, not a people management role. You will influence decisions through partnership with executive stakeholders. You are expected to operate with independence, strong judgment, and cross-functional collaboration. ABOUT YOU. You have 8+ years of relevant experience, or equivalent depth. Your background is in: Strategic Finance, FP&A, Investment Banking, or similar. You have strong experience with: Deep proficiency in Excel / Google Sheets. Experience working cross-functionally with business teams. Strong communication skills—able to translate complexity into clarity. You are comfortable operating in fast-paced, evolving environments. STRONG CANDIDATES MAY HAVE. Experience in DTC or CPG businesses. Familiarity with marketing performance analytics. Experience modeling fulfillment or operational cost structures. CLASSIFICATION & COMPENSATION. This temporary, full-time, exempt position is remote for candidates based in the following states: California, Maine, Pennsylvania, Nevada, North Carolina, Texas, Colorado, Washington, Illinois, New York, Missouri, and Massachusetts. The salary range for this role is $135,000-150,000. Our compensation bands are determined based on market-specific data, meaning that the top of our salary range is reserved for the most experienced candidates in the highest cost of living areas across the country. title: Inbound Supervisor
raw_description: The Inbound Operations Supervisor is responsible for overseeing daily inbound warehouse operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and accuracy across receiving, inventory handling, and dock operations. This role supervises a large team and partners cross-functionally to meet operational and service goals. At Grove, this role is all about being hands-on, steady, and reliable in execution —someone who can lead a team through the -day while keeping operations running smoothly and safely. Own the -day: You’ll lead a defined area of inbound operations, managing workflows that are well-established but require attention, consistency, and strong follow-through. Apply what works: You’ll rely on proven processes and playbooks, using your experience to guide decisions and support your team in getting the work done right

raw_description: Grove Collaborative is a sustainability-focused consumer products company creating household and personal care essentials that are effective, beautifully designed, and healthier for people and the planet. We are a certified B Corp, plastic-neutral, and on a mission to transform the CPG industry for good.. Grove is hiring Warehouse Associates at our Reno Fulfillment Center!. Shifts available:. Full-time: Friday–Sunday, 5:00 AM – 5:30 PM. Part-time: Friday and Sunday, 5:00 AM – 5:30 PM (2 - select days). Pay: $18.00 per hour, plus a $2.00 shift differential for Friday–Sunday shifts.. Full-time roles: New hires for this position are eligible for a retention bonus of up to $400 after 90 days of employment. Part-time roles: New hires for this position are eligible for a retention bonus of up to $200 after 90 days of employment.. At Grove Collaborative, we want you to bring your authentic self to work each day and feel supported. Wear the clothes you’re comfortable in and listen to the soundtrack of your new career in your headphones while you work!. Job Responsibilities & Requirements:. Unload containers of product from trucks in the Inbound area of the warehouse. You will be trained to be in either the picking or packing production areas. When in picking or packing position in the warehouse, you will be trained to meet daily performance goals and targets for customer orders using an RF scanner or Pack customer orders quickly, securely, efficiently, and label boxes for shipping using computer. Physical Requirements and Work Environment include: Ability to stand, walk, bend, squat, on a continual basis for shifts of 12 hours or longer per day, be able to push/pull carts and lift, pull or push up to 50 lbs. during a shift, be able to reach above shoulder level frequently, be able to work in a warehouse environment where temperatures vary with the seasons and be willing to work around equipment, forklifts, large trucks, and loud noise. We require that you are at least 18 years of age, are legally able to work in the United States and are willing to submit a background check report. Why Grove:. Set Schedules for Part Time Hours. PTO (paid time off) accrues from day one; up to hours a year. Generous Employee Referral Program; bring a friend and earn cash up to $450. Casual dress code, listen to your music while you work. Invest in your career with our in-house training programs, opportunities for advancement. Safe, clean, and organized warehouse, it smells good too!.
