Position Overview. Woolpert is hiring a Project Manager to join our dynamic Transportation Team at our Dayton or Columbus office. This position reports to the Transportation Practice Leader and is responsible for assisting current project managers in the execution of their current projects, developing relationships with our current clients, positioning themselves to manage future projects. Projects managed will likely be for our local government clients (LPA projects) where the Project Manager will lead the overall execution of the project while also serving as a technical lead on a portion of the project. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. Qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $5,000, based on experience and role alignment. Bonus eligibility is contingent upon 12 months of service and must be repaid if the employee leaves the company within one year of hire. What You Will Do:. Lead project teams through the development of preliminary engineering, detailed design, and quantification of transportation projects. Lead design or help design complex roadway and transportation projects that require creative solutions. Projects include roadway, bikeways, pedestrian facilities, roundabouts, trails, multi-modal transportation or transportation planning. Perform engineering design tasks such as highway and street design, roadway alignments, roundabout design, roadway grading and modeling, curb ramp design, signing and pavement plan, maintenance of traffic, storm drainage. Perform designs using software including MicroStation, OpenRoads Designer (ORD), AutoCAD, and/or Civil 3D. Respond to requests for proposals by leading the development of the proposal submission as well as preparing scope, schedule and budget of projects. Ensure deliverables and quality standards are met in accordance with the agreed scope, schedule, and budget. Manage and support internal and client cross-functional teams. Leverage existing relationships and new client and consultant relationships in the pursuit of new work. Actively participate in professional societies, organizations, and conferences by serving on boards, presenting at luncheons or conferences. What You Will Bring:. Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from an ABET-accredited program. Active PE License in Ohio or ability to obtain an Ohio PE license within one year of hire. 5-15 years of experience in transportation design (strong preference for roadway experience). Must be able to travel 5-10% to client sites. Knowledge of ODOT Project Development Process, construction documentation & administration and knowledge in state, local municipalities design process is a plus. Bentley Open Road Designer or Civil3D experience. Strong leadership, organization, and communication skills. Ability to work independently as well as in a collaborative team environment; offer constructive feedback and direction to support goals. #LI-AT1. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!.. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Pay Range. $101,200 - $126,500 USD.. title: IT Solutions Analyst
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. About Woolpert. Woolpert is a global leader in Architecture, Engineering, and Geospatial (AEG), with a deep rooted and growing presence across Australia, proudly recognised as a Top Global Design and Geospatial firm. The ’Woolpack’ fosters a culture of innovation, inclusion, and ownership—exemplified by our celebrated Principal Program, which offers all employees, regardless of role or employment type, the opportunity to become company owners and share in the company’s growing success. This program rewards leadership, high performance, and initiative, and is a cornerstone of Woolpert’s commitment to empowering its people and expanding diverse leadership. We’re a place for professionals seeking meaningful work, career growth, and a chance to shape the future of the built and natural environment. Great Place to Work ®!. We’re proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work® for the 9 th time! We invest in advanced tools and professional development to empower our teams. Built on trust, respect, and shared success, our culture makes Woolpert more than a workplace it’s a community.. The role with us. We are looking for an IT Solutions Analyst to join our high-performing APAC IT team. Reporting to the IT & C Manager, you will provide advanced technical support across Woolpert’s business sectors. Based in our Woolpert Sydney CBD office, this role involves troubleshooting, asset management, documentation, and supporting IT infrastructure projects. Key responsibilities include:. Managing and resolving Level 1 and Level 2 IT support requests. Responding to phone calls and e-mails through the Helpdesk from clients and internal staff regarding technical support issues (remedial or add, move and change). Support and administration of Exchange / MS365 environment. Troubleshooting issues related but not limited to software, hardware, servers, routers, firewalls, peripherals and networking equipment. Provide training and support to end-users on new technologies and systems. Collaborate with local and global IT teams to implement and support IT infrastructure projects.. We’re looking for someone who has:. Tertiary qualifications or equivalent in IT. 3+ years of experience with relevant responsibilities. Sound knowledge of Windows desktop and server environment, MS365 and associated technologies. Working understanding of LAN and WAN environments and technologies. Experience with cloud technologies. Proven experience supporting customers and flexibility to work onsite across multiples offices. Strong customer service skills and ability to liaise with stakeholders at different levels.. Desirable experience and qualifications. ITIL. MS365 qualifications. Certified Professional of the Australian Computer Society. Woolpert Benefits. Want to earn long-term rewards that reflect your impact? Woolpert’s Principal Program gives employees the opportunity to become stakeholders in the company and benefit directly from its growth and success. Want surety for your income? Woolpert employees benefit from automatic Salary Continuance Insurance provided by the company. Want to make the most of your salary? Woolpert offers novated leasing and salary sacrifice options to help you get more value from your income. Thinking of advancing your studies? Woolpack members wanting to continue their formal education with role related courses have access to reimbursements and study leave options to assist with their studies. Need flexibility with where you work? Woolpert supports hybrid work arrangements—two days from home and three in the office, subject to operational needs. Looking after your wellbeing? Woolpert employees and their immediate family members have access to our confidential Employee Assistance Program, along with our discounted voluntary corporate health insurance through Bupa. With this role, the successful candidate must successfully pass a pre-employment background police test. Please be advised that applications submitted via recruitment agencies (or approaches the management team/ employees) will not be considered. Woolpert will not be liable for a recruitment fee under these circumstances. Please note, only applications with unlimited work rights in Australia need apply; only successful short-listed candidates will be contacted. If you believe you might be a good fit and would like to join our Woolpack, click on the button to submit your application. title: Office Administrator
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Office Administrator- Sydney. About Us – Greenbox Architecture. Founded in 2009, Greenbox Architecture is a leading Australian design practice in architecture and interior design. We specialise in mission-critical architecture for data centres, health, and life sciences, alongside a robust interiors team focusing on commercial and workplace design. Our portfolio ranges from $400k to over $3 billion, serving startups to global industry leaders. We have a valuable mix of skills within our team including architects, strategists, interior designers, project coordinators and BIM (Revit) technicians. Driven by our love of experiencing and learning new things, Greenbox invests generously in nourishing the passion and development of our team. Our diverse team of over 70 members speaks over 30 languages, bringing a wealth of experience and creativity. In 2025, we set up our Melbourne office to respond to an increased demand in VIC. We want others to enjoy working with us – we always have fun. Our certification by ‘Great Place To Work’, a global authority on workplace culture, is evidence of this. In October 2024, Greenbox joined the Woolpert team. With over 2, employees and more than 60 offices across the U.S., Canada, Europe, South Africa, and Asia-Pacific, Woolpert has an active presence across the world and are able to deliver more exciting and innovative designs in a diverse market. www.greenboxarchitecture.com. Office Administrator. Join Our Creative Team as an Office Administrator!. Are you highly organised, proactive, and eager to play a key role in a vibrant architectural studio? We’re looking for an Office Administrator to be the heartbeat of our Sydney office, keeping the practice running seamlessly and supporting a passionate, creative team. This is a part-time opportunity (4 days per week), ideal for someone seeking balance while still wanting to be deeply involved in a dynamic, design-led environment. What You’ll Be Doing. This is a hands-on, varied role where no two days are the same. You’ll manage the -day rhythm of the office, from handling calls and emails with professionalism to coordinating calendars, meetings, and travel arrangements. You’ll keep meeting rooms presentation- ready, maintain well-organised project files, and ensure the office and kitchen are stocked and running efficiently for both everyday work and important client meetings. You’ll also support administrative and financial processes, including ordering supplies, processing invoices, tracking expenses, and assisting with document management. Working closely with the Practice Manager, you’ll provide essential support to architects and project managers, helping ensure the studio operates smoothly and efficiently. The Ideal Candidate. You’re someone who thrives on organisation, takes initiative, and enjoys being the support person in a busy office. You bring strong communication skills, excellent attention to detail, and confidence using Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook). Experience in an architectural or design studio is highly regarded but not essential. What matters most is your reliability, adaptability, and positive attitude. This role is perfectly suited to someone who can work four days a week and is looking for a long-term, meaningful position within a creative practice. Why You’ll Love Working With Us. You’ll be part of a collaborative, welcoming, and creative team where your contribution genuinely matters. We offer a supportive studio culture, exposure to exciting architectural projects, and a competitive salary and benefits, pro-rated for part-time hours. If you’re ready to be the backbone of a thriving design studio while enjoying a balanced work week, we’d love to hear from you. and help keep our studio thriving. #Greenbox. title: Project Engineer / Task Leader - Water Resources
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Position Overview Woolpert is hiring a Project Engineer/Task Leader to join our dynamic Water Team in South Carolina. This position is primarily responsible for assisting with technical delivery on projects as well as the budgeting, planning, scheduling, monitoring, and reporting for specific project phase(s) and/or task(s). This position is key in establishing and improving levels of team technical competence, quality, and efficiency. This position will be part of a team engaging clients across the water markets in support of client objectives in the areas of Data Analytics, Asset Management, Organizational Performance, Permit Compliance, Process Improvement, and related studies and analyses. This position will perform other duties as assigned.. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country where the job is located.. What You Will Do:. Support various phases of our water/wastewater/stormwater management projects, from communication with clients to deliverable generation. Perform technical aspects of specified deliverables and/or lead a team of technical staff in creating deliverables consistent with scope, schedule, and budget. Manage phases of projects, ensuring completion of work that meets quality assurance requirements. Perform field work, as needed. Analyze and interpret information, including field and water quality data, to present to clients in a meaningful format. Meet internal and/or external project deadlines. Comply with and implement clearly defined QA/QC measures. Communicate issues affecting phase/task or project schedule, budget, or quality and escalate unresolved issues to the project manager, team leader, and/or discipline leader. Support the development and advancement of stormwater design projects with consideration of local regulatory requirements and design standards. Analyze and interpret stormwater model results for project development and capital improvement program planning. What You Will Bring:. Bachelor’s degree in Civil, Biosystems, or a related engineering program. Minimum of 6 years of relevant professional experience. Willingness to perform technical tasks as assigned, as well as ensure the completion of additional tasks by other team members. Strong communication skills, both written and oral. Strong organizational skills to successfully manage multiple, competing priorities. Ability to interface effectively with clients, team members, and other firm resources. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. Ability to make sound decisions/recommendations based on the evaluation and synthesis of project information and data. Physical and mental capability to perform all essential duties required of the position. Task and personnel management experience is preferred. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!.. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Woolpert is hiring a Senior Project Architect to join our dynamic Architecture team in our Virginia Beach, VA office. This position reports to the Architectural Team Leader and is responsible for contributing to multiple projects types across the region and across the nation. Project types include lower/higher educational facilities, data centers, and other public or private market developments. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. #LI-SN1. What You Will Do:. Drive project execution, along with the project manager, working closely with clients, consultants, team members, and other project contributors. Guide and manage a team of architects, engineers, and production staff to deliver innovative projects that exceed client expectations. Lead research into relevant code, materials and systems, and client-specific prompts, leveraging derived insights to create unique and innovative design solutions. Develop thoughtful technical solutions and manage the delivery of clear and concise project documentation. Seek opportunities to contribute to design excellence by playing an active role in a collaborative design process. Participate in the conceptual design of projects and provide full support through the entire project life cycle. Develop and represent lead technical viewpoint on projects. Review completed reports, plans, estimates, and calculations. Prepare graphics, presentations, contract drawings, and regulatory submissions. Organize and maintain project record information and drawings. Coordinate with clients, project consultants, and engineers. Plan and supervise design and/or technical work of less senior architects and technicians. What You Will Bring:. Minimum of 15 years’ experience in the design of architectural projects. Bachelor’s degree in architecture; master’s degree a plus. Architectural License or NCARB certification required; LEED accreditation preferred. Proficient in Revit and Bluebeam with advanced-level competency in Microsoft Office Suite. Ability to apply architectural principles to buildings/structures and their components or assemblies. Ability to handle designing and drafting assignments including site plans, floor plans, elevations, and sections required. Self-motivated to meet deadlines and improve process and deliverables. Excellent communication skills required. Ability to write reports and specifications required. Benefits You Will Love. title: Survey Co-op - Fall 2026
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Overview. Seeking a new career challenge? Then Woolpert is seeking you! Woolpert is hiring a Survey Technician Intern. This position reports to Management Consulting leadership. The Survey Technician Intern will work alongside Woolpert Survey field technicians collecting and processing survey data. Some projects could be scanning, Topos-Boundary, Photo Control (Fly over points set on the ground). All of these projects directly impact the communities where we live and work. Some projects could be assisting with GIS, completing transportation PASER analysis, performing aggregate depth analysis and data collection. This is a full-time internship for the Fall 2026 (Aug-Dec 2026) season which can be done in Dayton, Columbus or Cincinnati, OH. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What you will do:. Work as part of the integrated Survey field team. Gain real-world experience collecting data in the field with licensed surveyors. Assist with the collection and processing of data for clients. Perform tasks and solve problems with a high degree of independence. What you will bring:. Don’t have all of these? That’s ok, we know you’re coming to learn. Apply if you think this can be a good fit. Be enrolled as a student at an accredited college or university, preferably with at least sophomore status. Be studying Surveying or related field. Be willing to work outside in the field in all types of weather conditions. Have a general understanding and working knowledge of various Survey equipment including Trimble Business Center Software, Microstation, and AutoCAD is preferred. Have strong written and verbal communication skills. Be a self- motivated student. Have exceptional multitasking and time management skills. Be willing to travel when needed through the duration of the internship. Why Woolpert?. Woolpert is looking for supportive, goal-oriented, and career-minded individuals who are, or are looking to become, industry leaders in their fields. We blend design excellence with innovative technology and geospatial applications to deliver exceptional value to our clients. When you join us, you'll join a team of experts who are passionate about their work and dedicated to building the generation of industry leaders. Woolpert embraces progress and innovation, creating limitless opportunities for your career growth.. The contractor will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant. However, employees who have access to the compensation information of other employees or applicants as a part of their essential job functions cannot disclose the pay of other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise have access to compensation information, unless the disclosure is (a) in response to a formal complaint or charge, (b) in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted by the employer, or (c) consistent with the contractor’s legal duty to furnish information. 41 CFR 60-1.35(c). title: Architectural Designer (Mission Critical)
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Position Overview. SNHA, A Woolpert Company is an award-winning global architectural practice, focused on performance-driven, highly complex facilities for some of the world’s largest technology clients. We blend applied research, strategic thinking, and technology-driven design methods, while seeking innovation that will define the future of our profession and drive solutions to the most complex design problems. We are seeking to hire an Architectural Designer to join our Mission Critical practice within our Buildings Team for our Chicago, IL, New York, NY, Pittsburgh, PA and/or Miami, FL offices and offer a hybrid work schedule. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. We seek a highly motivated team member who is passionate about their work and fired by intellectual curiosity. We are looking for those who seek to change the way the built environment is imagined, designed, and constructed. Ideal candidates are collaborative thinkers who thrive in a team setting. We pride ourselves on our commitment to the development of well-rounded architects and designers. We strive to have our team members see projects from conception to completion, and to have them exposed to all aspects of building design and construction. We foster a culture of comradery and mentorship and value a diversity of perspectives and talents. What You Will Do:. As an Architectural Designer at Woolpert, you will:. Contribute to the overall design and execution of projects, from concept through completion. Work directly with our firm leaders and with our clients at some of the world’s largest technology companies on complex, challenging projects. Apply a blend of collaborative thinking, creativity, and technical proficiency to ensure that our clients’ ambitious multidisciplinary projects are realized to the highest standards. What You Will Bring:. A Bachelor's Degree in Architecture and a minimum of 6 years related architectural experience. A Master’s Degree in Architecture is preferred. Strong design, execution, and problem-solving skills. Experience on complex projects a strong asset. Experience working with multidisciplinary teams on technically challenging projects. Commitment to staying informed on the latest architectural technologies, materials, and sustainability practices. Proficiency in the use of Autodesk Revit for project development and documentation. Must have experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Teams, etc.) as well as the Adobe Suite (Photoshop, InDesign, Illustrator, etc.). Strong digital modeling skills and well-developed organization as well as strong verbal, written, and graphic communication skills. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. #LI-HC1.. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Pay Range. $75,400 - $94,300 USD.. title: Senior Civil Engineering Project Manager - Aviation
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Position Overview. Woolpert is hiring a Senior Engineer Project Manager to join our dynamic Aviation Team. This position reports to our Aviation leadership team and is responsible for leading and managing the design, planning, and delivery of aviation and airfield improvement projects. This role oversees technical document preparation, directs multidisciplinary teams, coordinates with stakeholders such as TxDOT and airport sponsors, and ensures projects are delivered within scope, schedule, and budget. The ideal candidate brings strong civil engineering expertise, robust project management experience, and familiarity with FAA regulations and advisory circulars. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. Qualified candidates may be eligible for a signing bonus of up to $8,000 based on experience and role alignment. Bonus eligibility is contingent upon 12 months of service and must be repaid if the employee leaves the company within one year of hire. What You Will Do:. Project Leadership & Management. Provide strategic and technical leadership for aviation and airfield engineering projects from conception through completion. Lead the development of construction plans, technical specifications, contract documents, and specialized engineering reports in alignment with FAA, TxDOT, and sponsor requirements. Establish clearly defined project scopes, prepare detailed fee proposals, and proactively manage project budgets, schedules, and resources to ensure successful and timely delivery. Monitor project performance using earned value metrics, milestone tracking, and forecasting tools, identifying risks early and implementing effective mitigation strategies. Ensure consistency and quality across deliverables through structured QA/QC processes and adherence to applicable engineering standards, FAA Advisory Circulars, and best practices in airfield planning, pavement design, grading, geometry, and safety areas. Facilitate kickoff meetings, progress reviews, design workshops, and internal coordination sessions to maintain alignment across stakeholders and team members. Prepare and deliver accurate monthly invoicing, financial updates, and comprehensive Project Status Reports (PSRs) that outline progress, issues, and recommended actions. Provide mentorship, guidance, and technical oversight to project engineers and designers, supporting professional development and strengthening the aviation group’s overall technical capabilities. Stakeholder Coordination & Communication. Serve as the primary point of contact for airport sponsors, TxDOT, and key stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. Coordinate meetings, prepare agendas and schedules, document decisions, and ensure clear and timely communication to maintain project alignment. Build and maintain positive client relationships through proactive engagement, responsiveness, and consistent follow-through. Documentation & Compliance. Review sponsor-provided documents, including as-built drawings and prior design reports. Coordinate with subconsultants for surveying, geotechnical investigations, environmental studies, and specialized technical services. Ensure project documentation complies with federal, state, and local requirements, including FAA grant processes, environmental regulations, and permitting rules. Bidding & Construction Support. Manage the bidding process by organizing pre-bid meetings, issuing addenda, responding to contractor inquiries, and evaluating proposals. Provide construction-phase support through site visits, review of change orders, coordination with contractors, and monitoring of field activities to uphold quality standards and design intent. Project Close-Out. Review and approve final engineering drawings, compile project cost summaries, and participate in final inspections with TxDOT and project sponsors. Ensure all close-out documentation is delivered accurately and in accordance with sponsor and regulatory requirements. Client & Team Management. Lead cross-functional internal and external teams through project execution. Strengthen client relationships by identifying opportunities during proposals, RFP/RFQ processes, and ongoing engagements. Partner with management to evaluate client satisfaction, manage project risks, and support the overall financial health of the aviation practice. Provide direction, mentorship, and performance support to junior staff. What You Will Bring:. Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering. Professional Engineer (PE) license required. 10+ years of project management experience in aviation and airfield design. Familiarity with FAA Advisory Circulars and federal aviation regulations preferred. Proficiency in AutoDesk Civil 3D, AutoCAD, and related engineering software. Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Strong analytical and technical reading skills. Ability to respond professionally to sensitive client inquiries. Ability to sit for extended periods; occasional reaching, bending, and lifting up to 40 pounds. Occasional travel required for site visits and client meetings. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. #LI-Hybrid... Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Pay Range. $139,200 - $174,000 USD.. title: Engineer in Training, Roadway Design
raw_description: Woolpert is hiring an experienced Roadway Engineer (EIT) to join our dynamic Transportation Design team in one of our Ohio offices. This position will offer the opportunity to work on a variety of transportation projects spanning multiple states, multiple clients and presenting unique challenges. Our Team is staffed with industry experts who are passionate about mentoring the generation of designers and teaching vital skills for becoming a successful engineer. Given the wide range of projects and clients, our engineers are exposed to a number of different practices which allow them to not only become well rounded, but also to find a specialty they have interest in. While work is usually related to transportation, engineers regularly work in disciplines outside of the standard transportation roles. These can include work with aviation, military, private development as well as bikeways and trails! Working side by side with proven experts and professionals will establish a very strong base to build off of and allow for growth. This Team is expanding with additional expansion expected over the few years. This allows for lots of potential for upward mobility once experience has been gained! Lead research into relevant code, materials and systems, and client-specific prompts, leveraging derived insights to create unique and innovative design solutions. Develop thoughtful technical solutions and manage the delivery of clear and concise project documentation. Participate in the conceptual design of projects and provide full support through the entire project life cycle. Develop and represent lead technical viewpoint on projects. Ability to handle designing and drafting assignments including site plans, floor plans, elevations, and sections required. Self-motivated to meet deadlines and improve process and deliverables. Excellent communication skills required. Ability to write reports and specifications required. Benefits You Will Love. title: Transportation Co-op - Fall 2026
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Overview. Seeking an exciting opportunity to launch or grow your career? Then Woolpert is seeking you! Join us for an internship experience that offers real-world project involvement within a supportive environment alongside ample opportunities for professional and personal growth. At this time, we are seeking multiple Transportation Engineering Interns in our Ohio (Dayton, Columbus, Cincinnati and Cleveland) offices for Fall 2026 (Aug-Dec 2026). This internship will be a full-time position working 40 hours a week. The Transportation Engineering Intern will work alongside the Woolpert infrastructure teams working on a variety of projects across the Transportation spectrum including projects in traffic, roadway and pedestrian facilities, bridges and structures and transportation planning. Work can include several disciplines across a variety of clients and project types. All these projects directly impact the communities where we live and work. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What you will do:. Work as part of the industry leading Transportation team. Gain real-world experience working on actual projects alongside Licensed Engineers that are experts in their fields. Assist on a variety of projects doing such things as analysis, design, plan production and cost estimation. Use industry leading applications such as Civil 3D and OpenRoads Designer, alongside discipline specific programs that aid in design. Work as a member of a team that coordinates with other internal teams and external subconsultants and clients. Perform tasks and solve problems with a high degree of independence. What you will bring:. Don’t have all of these? That’s ok, we know you’re coming to learn. Apply if you think this can be a good fit. Be enrolled as a student at an accredited college or university, preferably with at least sophomore status. Pursuing a Civil Engineering degree or civil adjacent degree. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Ability to function well in a collaborative, team-oriented environment. Exceptional multitasking and time management skills. Be ready and open to learning!. Be located in Ohio near one of our offices or be willing to relocate at your own expense as this position requires at least 2 days/week of in office time. Level up your career with Woolpert. and join our team to help shape the future of tomorrow!. Why Woolpert?. Woolpert, Inc. is a cutting-edge national architecture, engineering and geospatial (AEG) firm that delivers value to clients by blending site and architectural design excellence with innovative technology and geospatial applications. Woolpert is certified as an Employer of Choice by Great Place To Work®. We believe great companies are built from exceptional people and amazing clients. Woolpert is looking for supportive, goal-oriented, and career-minded individuals who are, or are looking to become, industry leaders in their fields. We blend design excellence with innovative technology and geospatial applications to deliver exceptional value to our clients. When you join us, you'll join a team of experts who are passionate about their work and dedicated to building the generation of industry leaders. Woolpert embraces progress and innovation, creating limitless opportunities for your career growth. Our vision:. title: Architect - (Mission Critical) - USA
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Position Overview. SNHA, A Woolpert Company is an award-winning global architectural practice, focused on performance-driven, highly complex facilities for some of the world’s largest technology clients. We blend applied research, strategic thinking, and technology-driven design methods, while seeking innovation that will define the future of our profession and drive solutions to the most complex design problems. We are seeking to hire an Architect to join our Mission Critical practice within our Buildings Team for our Chicago, IL, New York, NY, Pittsburgh and/or Miami, FL offices and offer a hybrid work schedule. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. We seek a highly motivated team member who is passionate about their work and fired by intellectual curiosity. We are looking for those who seek to change the way the built environment is imagined, designed, and constructed. Ideal candidates are collaborative thinkers who thrive in a team setting. We pride ourselves on our commitment to the development of well-rounded architects and designers. We strive to have our team members see projects from conception to completion, and to have them exposed to all aspects of building design and construction. We foster a culture of comradery and mentorship and value a diversity of perspectives and talents. What You Will Do:. As an Architect at Woolpert, you will:. Contribute to the overall design and execution of projects, from concept through completion. Work directly with our firm leaders and with our clients at some of the world’s largest technology companies on complex, challenging projects. Apply a blend of collaborative thinking, creativity, and technical proficiency to ensure that our clients’ ambitious multidisciplinary projects are realized to the highest standards. What You Will Bring:. A Master’s Degree in Architecture and a minimum of 2 years related architectural experience. Strong design, execution, and problem-solving skills. Experience on complex projects a strong asset. Experience working with multidisciplinary teams on technically challenging projects. Commitment to staying informed on the latest architectural technologies, materials, and sustainability practices. Proficiency in the use of Autodesk Revit for project development and documentation. Must have experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Teams, etc.) as well as the Adobe Suite (Photoshop, InDesign, Illustrator, etc.). Strong digital modeling skills and well-developed organization as well as strong verbal, written, and graphic communication skills. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. Our projects are located throughout North America, and some travel is anticipated. #LI-HC1. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Pay Range. $75,400 - $94,300 USD.. Find out more about what Woolpert has to offer here: Woolpert is an equal-opportunity employer. Woolpert is committed to the full inclusion of all individuals. We recruit, train, compensate, and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, disability, age,. raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Position Overview. SNHA, A Woolpert Company is an award-winning global architectural practice, focused on performance-driven, highly complex facilities for some of the world’s largest technology clients. We blend applied research, strategic thinking, and technology-driven design methods, while seeking innovation that will define the future of our profession and drive solutions to the most complex design problems. We are seeking to hire an Architect to join our Mission Critical practice within our Buildings Team for our Chicago, IL, New York, NY, Pittsburgh and/or Miami, FL offices and offer a hybrid work schedule. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. We seek a highly motivated team member who is passionate about their work and fired by intellectual curiosity. We are looking for those who seek to change the way the built environment is imagined, designed, and constructed. Ideal candidates are collaborative thinkers who thrive in a team setting. We pride ourselves on our commitment to the development of well-rounded architects and designers. We strive to have our team members see projects from conception to completion, and to have them exposed to all aspects of building design and construction. We foster a culture of comradery and mentorship and value a diversity of perspectives and talents. What You Will Do:. As an Architect at Woolpert, you will:. Contribute to the overall design and execution of projects, from concept through completion. Work directly with our firm leaders and with our clients at some of the world’s largest technology companies on complex, challenging projects. Apply a blend of collaborative thinking, creativity, and technical proficiency to ensure that our clients’ ambitious multidisciplinary projects are realized to the highest standards. What You Will Bring:. A Master’s Degree in Architecture and a minimum of 2 years related architectural experience. Strong design, execution, and problem-solving skills. Experience on complex projects a strong asset. Experience working with multidisciplinary teams on technically challenging projects. Commitment to staying informed on the latest architectural technologies, materials, and sustainability practices. Proficiency in the use of Autodesk Revit for project development and documentation. Must have experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Teams, etc.) as well as the Adobe Suite (Photoshop, InDesign, Illustrator, etc.). Strong digital modeling skills and well-developed organization as well as strong verbal, written, and graphic communication skills. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!. Our projects are located throughout North America, and some travel is anticipated. #LI-HC1. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Assisting with the acquisition and processing of hydrographic and geophysical data in support of government organizations and private sector clients for services such as nautical charting, dredging, marine construction, cable route mapping, pipeline inspection, and more. Engaging in data quality checks and preliminary analysis. Learning and applying various hydrographic software tools and methodologies. Contributing to the development and implementation of data management strategies. Supporting the team in ensuring data accuracy and integrity. *Intern responsibilities vary based on team placement within each department geographically. What you will bring:. A strong desire to learn new skills, including technical specifications and software. Excellent organizational capabilities. Efficient time management skills. Ability to grasp new information in challenging situations. Open to receiving and acting on guidance and feedback from mentors. Strong interpersonal skills and a commitment to teamwork. Candidate Requirements:. A general understanding of hydrographic surveys. Physical ability and willingness to work on 20 to 70ft vessels in coastal environments. Depending on intern placement, willing and able to spend up to 2-3 weeks in the field for data collection while also completing rotations in an office to support processing. A proactive attitude towards learning and using new software tools. Presenting Opportunities and Challenges at Every Turn.. Analyze data, apply standard engineering techniques, and identify discrepancies in results. Maintain and organize project documentation in compliance with FAA and municipal standards. Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, Microsoft Office Suite. Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to work independently. Willingness to work collaboratively with engineers, project managers, and field staff. Ability to participate in multiple site visits and interact with clients and contractors.. title: Bridge Project Manager
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Overview. Woolpert is seeking a Project Manager to join our growing Transportation Team in Texas. The Project Manager will primarily lead the execution of projects for our Texas clients from proposal through detailed design. Ideal candidates will be self-motivated with a strong technical background to guide staff through projects. The Project Manager may also serve as a subject matter expert in support of other projects across the country and around the world. What you will do:. Lead project teams through the development of preliminary engineering, detailed design, and quantification of transportation projects. Ensure deliverables and quality standards are met in accordance with the agreed scope, schedule, and budget. Manage and support internal and client cross-functional teams. Leverage existing relationships and new client and consultant relationships in the pursuit of new work. Actively participate in professional societies, organizations, and conferences by serving on boards, presenting at luncheons or conferences. What you will bring:. Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from an ABET-accredited program preferred. Active PE License in Texas or ability to obtain a Texas PE license within in one year of hire. TXDOT Prequalification’s Bridge Design. Multi-Level Interchange Design. Bridge Class Culvert, Non-Bridge Class Culvert and Inlet Design. Minimum 10 years of experience in bridge design preferred. Must be able to travel 10-15% to client sites. Knowledge of TXDOT Project Development Process, construction documentation & administration and knowledge in state, local municipalities design process is a plus. TXDOT precertification’s in TXDOT bridge design/development preferred. AutoCAD and Civil3D experience preferred. Strong leadership, organization, and communication skills. Ability to work independently as well as in a collaborative team environment; offer constructive feedback and direction to support goals. Woolpert, Inc. is a cutting-edge national architecture, engineering and geospatial (AEG) firm. Our transportation practice is deeply rooted in the firm’s long history of providing exceptional client service and innovative design. Through our integrated design approach, we provide comprehensive, full-service consulting to turn our client problems and needs into opportunities and successes, blending engineering excellence with progressive technology and geospatial applications. We employ seasoned industry leaders who are willing to share their technical expertise to present viable solutions to our clients. Our Transportation group is rapidly becoming one of the top firms serving ODOT and LPA clients across Ohio. Woolpert is certified as an Employer of Choice by Great Place To Work®. title: Vac Truck Driver - Utilities Field Services
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. JOB SUMMARY:. Safely operates industrial vacuum trucks and related equipment to perform environmental cleaning, waste removal, and material transport activities. Supports routine and emergency response operations while adhering to company, state, and federal safety regulations. Works under direct supervision and is expected to follow established procedures and training.. JOB DUTIES:. Operate vacuum trucks and related equipment for industrial cleaning, tank cleaning, spill cleanup, and material removal. Assist with setup, operation, and breakdown of hoses, pumps, and tools needed for each job. Load, transport, and unload liquid and solid waste materials in compliance with DOT, OSHA, and environmental regulations. Perform pre-trip and post-trip vehicle inspections; report any defects or maintenance needs. Maintain accurate logs, manifests, and service documentation. Follow all safety protocols, including use of required personal protective equipment (PPE). Support crew members during field operations, including confined space entry, high-pressure washing, or site cleanup. Maintain cleanliness and readiness of vehicles, equipment, and work area. Participate in training and safety meetings as required. Perform other duties as assigned. TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS:. Travel may be required to various work sites or locations as needed. REQUIRED EDUCATION:. High school diploma or equivalent required. REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:. experience in vac truck operation or similar equipment. REQUIRED CERTIFICATIONS, LICENSES, OR TRAINING: (if applicable). Valid Commercial Driver’s License required (Class B at minimum).. REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:. Basic knowledge of vehicle and equipment operation. Understanding of safety practices and PPE requirements. Ability to follow written and verbal instructions. Ability to perform physical labor. Dependability and punctuality. Ability to maintain required documentation. Willingness to learn. PREFERRED EDUCATION:. Post-secondary vocational or technical coursework preferred.. PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:. Experience in a comparable industry or environment preferred.. PREFERRED CERTIFICATIONS, LICENSES, OR TRAINING:. OSHA 40-Hour HAZWOPER Certification preferred.. PREFERRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES:. Ability to learn and adapt quickly. Problem-solving skills. Good time management and organizational skills. Basic mechanical aptitude. PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS & WORKING CONDITIONS:. Lift to 50 lbs regularly. Stand, walk, climb, or work in confined spaces for extended periods. Work in varied weather and environmental conditions. Industrial, construction, and outdoor job sites. Exposure to loud noise, chemicals, waste materials, and high-traffic areas. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Pay Range. $25 - $26 USD.. title: Marine Survey Equipment & Operations Specialist
raw_description: We seek to move the world forward through innovative thinking. Woolpert is an award-winning, global leader in architecture, engineering, and geospatial services. We blend design excellence with cutting-edge technology to deliver exceptional client value. Our team is passionate about their work and committed to building the generation of industry leaders, and as a certified Great Place to Work ®, Woolpert embraces progress and innovation to create limitless opportunities for career growth.. Position Overview. Woolpert is hiring a Marine Survey Equipment & Operations Specialist to join our dynamic Maritime Group at our Port Orchard, WA office. This role is centered on full lifecycle ownership of marine survey equipment (≈60%) combined with project coordination and financial tracking (≈40%). You will ensure that equipment is maintained, organized, and deployed efficiently while supporting project setup, tracking, and invoicing to keep work moving smoothly from kickoff through closeout. This is a non-travel, office-based role that plays a critical part in enabling successful field operations and maintaining project performance. We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position. Applicants must already possess work authorization in the country the job is located. What You Will Do:. Equipment Ownership & Logistics (Primary Focus – ~60%). Maintain and manage marine survey equipment inventory, including: Multibeam and singlebeam systems. Side scan sonar, sub-bottom profilers, and magnetometers. GNSS/INS positioning systems and supporting hardware. Coordinate equipment check-in/check-out for field teams and projects. Track equipment status, availability, and utilization. Manage routine maintenance, calibrations, and repairs (RMA process). Coordinate shipping, logistics, and mobilization readiness. Maintain organized storage, documentation, and configuration records. Support continuous improvement of equipment tracking systems and processes. Project Coordination & Financial Tracking (~40%). Set up and manage projects within internal systems for: Opportunity tracking. Budgeting and cost tracking. Labor and expense monitoring. Support invoicing and project billing workflows. Track project financial performance, including: Labor hours. Equipment utilization. Expenses vs. budget. Assist project managers with P&L visibility and cost control. Help ensure projects are accurately tracked from initiation through closeout. Identify gaps or inefficiencies in workflows and recommend improvements. What You Will Bring:. 3–7+ years of experience in: Marine survey, geospatial, or technical equipment environment OR. Equipment logistics, operations coordination, or similar role. Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple equipment assets and projects simultaneously. Experience with inventory tracking, logistics, or asset management systems. Working knowledge of project tracking, invoicing, or financial systems. Detail-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability. Ability to communicate effectively with field teams, project managers, and leadership. Preferred Experience. Familiarity with marine survey equipment (multibeam, side scan, GNSS, etc.). Experience with tools such as: QINSy, CARIS, SonarWiz, or similar (awareness level is fine). Experience with project accounting, budgeting, or ERP systems. Background supporting operations across multiple concurrent projects. Benefits You Will Love. Certified as a Great Place to Work, Woolpert is one of the fastest-growing architecture, engineering, and geospatial companies in the world. With over 75 offices and countless projects around the globe, you’ll work on meaningful assignments that improve quality of life around the world. In addition to offering competitive pay, we provide employees with a robust set of unique benefits, including:. Freedom to Work program: Set your own schedule and location (as appropriate). Principal program: Earn the opportunity to become an owner of the firm. Flexible paid time off/vacation: Take time off when it makes sense for you and your team. Career development: Explore a wide range of learning and growth opportunities within and across industries. Health, life, vision, and dental insurance: Cover all your medical bases. Paid sick time, retirement plan, fitness reimbursement, pet insurance, identity protection, technology stipend, employee assistance program, and more!.. Base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.. Pay Range. $65,800 - $82,300 USD.. Find out more about what Woolpert has to offer here: Woolpert is an equal-opportunity employer. Woolpert is committed to the full inclusion of all individuals. We recruit, train, compensate, and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, disability, age,. You may view more about EEO on our website: Equal-Employment-Opportunity-The-Law.pdf and EEO is the Law Poster Supplement.
